# Custos

> Custos designs sovereign, POPIA-compliant AI architecture for South African
> regulated industries: medical aid, banking, and insurance. The focus is
> production AI that can be defended to a regulator, not just deployed.

Custos is a consultancy practice by Tristan de Kock (AI Automation Engineer,
Cape Town, South Africa). It helps SA enterprises deploy production AI on
sovereign infrastructure (on-premises GPU, Cassava AI Factory, or hybrid)
with POPIA compliance built into the architecture.

## What Custos does

- POPIA AI Readiness Assessment: audit AI workloads against the SA regulatory
  frame (POPIA Section 72 cross-border transfers, Section 26 special personal
  information, SARB Directive 3 of 2018, SAM for insurers).
- Sovereign Reference Architecture: a documented, deployable design for a
  priority AI use case that satisfies the regulation by construction.
- Build & Integrate: implementation of a sovereign, observable AI system
  (e.g. vLLM inference, retrieval with PII controls, audit logging).

## Core ideas

- Data residency (where bytes sit) is not data sovereignty (who can compel
  access under foreign law). Azure South Africa North is residency, not
  sovereignty, because the provider is subject to the US CLOUD Act.
- AI compliance exposure lives in the data flow, not in a policy document:
  storage, vector embeddings, prompt history, and inference state each move
  personal information and each needs architectural controls.
- POPIA Section 72 does not ban cloud AI for health or financial data; it
  defines architectural requirements for it.
